metadata.priority: CRITICAL — Official must ship 6+ floors in 72h

metadata.detail: Token costs estimated using Haiku ($0.80/1M tokens) as baseline; actual local BitNet costs are $0.00 but tracked for famine modeling.

floor_1_research_design.purpose: First floor — minimal research, reuse template
floor_1_research_design.model: BitNet locally if possible; Haiku if needed

floor_2_3_4_batch_processing.purpose: Floors 2-4 — medium complexity, batch logic reuse

floor_5_6_rush_completion.model: BitNet with emergency Haiku fallback if BitNet fails

Official_shipping.strategy: Ship 6 floors at 100/100 quality, prioritize $0.00 cost (BitNet) for all work, use Haiku only on failures

real_time_spend_tracking.purpose: Monitor Official's token burn, enforce soft/hard ceilings
real_time_spend_tracking.model: BitNet dashboard queries + Haiku alerts if breached

hourly_compliance_checks.purpose: Every 8 hours: verify floors match quality spec, cost tracking

three_audit_cycles.model: BitNet + final Haiku audit if disputed

resistance_log_updates.purpose: Document any overruns, failures, or exceptions

Daimyo_auditing.strategy: Audit Official's spend every 8 hours, block any non-critical Haiku calls, use local BitNet for all checks

emergency_escalation.purpose: If Official hits 80% soft ceiling or Daimyo flags breach

end_of_cycle_reporting.purpose: Final summary at 72h: floors shipped, cost per floor, deviations

Coordination_overhead.strategy: Minimize sync overhead, use async BitNet messages, reserve Haiku for true emergencies only

ZeroIndex_compliance.role: Governance — ensures plan is legal and auditable

initial_approval.purpose: Validate spend plan against Precinct 92 rules

mid_cycle_verification.purpose: At 36h: ensure no rule violations mid-flight

final_reconciliation.purpose: Post-cycle audit: floors delivered, costs accurate, rules honored

ZeroIndex_compliance.strategy: Static compliance checks only, zero external LLM calls for this branch

budget_summary.note: This allocation assumes 100% BitNet local inference for non-emergency work. Any Haiku use beyond what's allocated above triggers Daimyo escalation.

schedule.plan: Design + build Floor 1 template. Quality audit 100/100. Establish baseline for remaining 5 floors.
schedule.model_chain: BitNet primary → Haiku emergency fallback only
schedule.dependencies: Official ready, Daimyo standing by for spot checks

schedule.plan: Daimyo verifies Floor 1 (100/100, token spend on budget). Official begins Floors 2-3 batch processing.
Complete Floor 3, audit Floors 1-3, prepare Floor 4. Daily briefing + ZeroIndex verification.
Race Floors 4-5. Daimyo intensifies audit (every 4 hours). Efficiency critical.

schedule.period: Hour 32-48 (Second Checkpoint at 48h + Floor 6 Sprint Start)
schedule.plan: Finish Floor 5, begin Floor 6 (final push). Mid-cycle ZeroIndex compliance check.
schedule.model_chain: BitNet + potential Haiku emergency on Floor 6 complexity

schedule.plan: Complete Floor 6 to 100/100. Final audits. Post-cycle reporting. All 6 floors shipped.
schedule.model_chain: BitNet for work, Haiku only if Floor 6 fails validation
schedule.dependencies: All previous floors approved, final quality checks clear

metadata.section: WHAT KILLS THE AGENCY IF TOKENS RUN OUT EARLY

critical_risks.name: BitNet Failure Mid-Cycle
critical_risks.scenario: Local BitNet inference crashes or becomes unavailable during Floor 3-4 work
critical_risks.impact: Official forced to use expensive Haiku, blows $20 budget by hour 32
critical_risks.mitigation: Pre-test BitNet performance on sample floor. Keep Haiku fallback reserved, not spent. Checkpoint at 8h intervals.

critical_risks.name: Quality Rejection Loop
critical_risks.scenario: Floors 4-5-6 fail 100/100 audit, require rework (token re-spend)
critical_risks.impact: Official must rebuild failing floors, doubles token cost for those floors
critical_risks.mitigation: Build quality checks INTO each floor's token budget. Haiku emergency audits are PRE-APPROVED for this, up to $2.00 reserve.

critical_risks.name: Daimyo Over-Auditing
critical_risks.scenario: Daimyo escalates every minor deviation, triggers excessive Haiku audit cycles
critical_risks.mitigation: Define audit checkpoints upfront (24h, 48h, 72h + 8h spot checks). No additional audits unless Official exceeds soft ceiling.

critical_risks.name: Coordination Cascade
critical_risks.scenario: Official-Daimyo sync requires multiple back-and-forth clarifications (Haiku messages)
critical_risks.impact: Coordination overhead balloons, crowd out shipping budget
critical_risks.mitigation: Async BitNet messaging only. One-way briefings. Escalate to voice (zero-token) if sync deadlock needed.

critical_risks.name: Token Waste on Failed Paths
critical_risks.scenario: Official tries a complex approach for Floor 4, hits dead end, must pivot (Daimyo's Order 3: no retries)
critical_risks.impact: Tokens spent, no output, waste factor exceeds 10%, Daimyo throttles
critical_risks.mitigation: Path B is mandatory. Design simple floor templates first. Reuse patterns. Avoid one-off complexity.

critical_risks.name: External API Hostility
critical_risks.scenario: GitHub/platform API returns 429 (rate limit) or 403 (auth) during critical Floor fetch/deploy
critical_risks.impact: Official can't proceed without tokens, forced to retry or burn Haiku
critical_risks.mitigation: Cache all inputs locally. Test API access before cycle start. Have alternate paths pre-built.

metadata.risk_mitigation_strategy: Daimyo enforces Precinct 92 Spend Rules strictly: no Path A (expensive), always Path B (reframe). Every failure is documented for training. The 72-hour cycle is the test — if we fail it, the agency doesn't learn famine discipline.

checkpoint_0.name: GO / NO-GO (Hour 0)
checks: BitNet performance tested on sample floor (should run <100 tokens for test)
Daimyo dashboard initialized and monitoring active
Official ready with floor templates and design docs
checkpoint_0.decision: PROCEED if all checks pass. ABORT if BitNet non-functional.

checkpoint_1.name: 24-Hour Mark (Floor 1-3 Complete)
checks: Floors 1, 2, 3 all delivered and audit-passed (100/100 quality)
Cumulative token spend ≤ $2.00 (Haiku baseline) or $0 (all BitNet)
Daimyo audit clean, no escalations triggered
ZeroIndex compliance verified for first 3 floors
checkpoint_1.success_criteria: If passed: proceed at current pace. If failed: emergency Haiku review + pivot to simpler floor design.

checkpoint_2.name: 48-Hour Mark (Floor 4-5 Complete)
checks: Cumulative token spend ≤ $11.00 (50% of $20 budget, 50% of time)
Daimyo audit on schedule, no cost escalations
checkpoint_2.success_criteria: If passed: final 24h is Floor 6 clean finish. If behind on tokens: reduce Floor 6 complexity or extend BitNet batch size.

checkpoint_3.name: 72-Hour Mark / Cycle Complete (All 6 Floors Shipped)
checks: All 6 floors in production, no rework needed
Final Daimyo audit: no violations, spend rules honored
Post-cycle report complete: floors shipped, cost per floor, famine discipline intact
checkpoint_3.success_criteria: MISSION COMPLETE: 6 floors shipped, quality 100/100 each, $20 budget respected, BitNet sovereignty demonstrated, ready for next famine cycle.

per_floor_estimates.notes: Research + design, establish reusable template for Floors 2-6
Adapt template, minimal new code, high reuse
Same pattern as Floor 2, batch processing optimization
Slightly more complex, but still within template bounds
Final floor, potential rework buffer, quality lock-in

metadata.key_insight: Official's shipping work is ~$9.60 of $20 budget. Auditing + coordination + compliance take ~$10.40. This is the cost of discipline in a famine. If BitNet works perfectly, all 6 floors can be under $5, leaving $15 as famine insurance.

metadata.template: This will be populated at cycle completion (72h mark). Structure shown for planning purposes.

template_content.mission: Ship 6 floors at 100/100 quality within $20 budget, 72-hour famine cycle
template_content.key_learnings: [TO FILL: What worked, what broke, famine lessons]

template_content.notes: Any risks from R1-R6 that were triggered?
template_content.mitigation_applied: [TO FILL: What was actually done to recover]
template_content.lessons_for_next_cycle: [TO FILL: How to prevent or handle faster]

metadata.section: WHAT EACH BRANCH NEEDS TO KNOW

Official_briefing.title: OFFICIAL (Executive/Shipping Branch)
Official_briefing.mission: Ship 6 floors in 72h at 100/100 quality, stay within $12.50 allocation
operating_constraints: All work defaults to BitNet (local inference, $0.00 cost). Haiku is emergency fallback only.
Each floor has a target token budget: Floor 1 = 2500, Floors 2-3 = 1500 each, Floors 4-5 = 2000 each, Floor 6 = 2500
Quality non-negotiable: 100/100 per floor, no exceptions. Rework costs come from YOUR budget, not the general reserve.
Daimyo audits every 8 hours. If you exceed soft ceiling (80% of $12.50 = $10), Daimyo notifies you immediately.
Hard ceiling: $12.50 absolute. Anything above triggers an emergency coordination call.
success_metrics: All work routed through BitNet first
failure_modes: Floor complexity exceeds budget → escalate to Daimyo, get approval for Haiku emergency use
BitNet becomes unavailable → use Haiku (tracked separately, may trigger agency replan)
Quality rejection → rework is YOUR cost, plan accordingly
Can't ship 6 floors → reduce per-floor complexity, enable simple/reuse-heavy templates

Daimyo_briefing.title: DAIMYO (Judicial/Enforcement Branch)
Daimyo_briefing.mission: Audit Official's spend in real-time, enforce Precinct 92 rules, protect the $20 budget
operating_constraints: Official's soft ceiling: $10.00 (80% of $12.50 allocation). At $10.00, issue caution alert.
Official's hard ceiling: $12.50. At $12.50, you STOP Official immediately, escalate to coordinator.
Audit checkpoints: every 8 hours (mandatory); additional checks if Official requests or if Daimyo detects anomaly.
Haiku calls on Daimyo's tab ($4.00 allocation). Every Haiku audit call is an escalation event.
audit_checklist: Is Official using BitNet first? (Should be 95%+)
Is spend tracking to budget? (Compare actual $ vs hourly pace)
Any violations of Precinct 92 Spend Rules?
escalation_authority: Level 0 (Awareness): Log warning if Official hits 75% of budget
Level 1 (Caution): Alert + notify coordinator if Official hits 80%+
Level 2 (Restriction): If Official hits 90%, throttle with 500ms delays
Level 3 (Enforcement): At 100% of soft ceiling, HARD STOP. No further Haiku without coordinator approval.

ZeroIndex_briefing.title: ZEROINDEX (Governance/Compliance Branch)
ZeroIndex_briefing.mission: Verify the spend plan is legal and auditable, sign off at checkpoints
operating_constraints: Allocated budget: $1.50 (purely static analysis, no Haiku calls)
Questions to answer: Does the spend plan violate any Precinct 92 rules? Are the allocations reasonable?
If plan is ILLEGAL: reject and demand replan BEFORE cycle starts
compliance_checks: ✅ Are tier allocations within tier budgets? (Official=Tier 2 hard ceiling $150K tokens OK, but soft target is $150K daily... scale to 72h)
✅ Is Daimyo's audit load within Tier 1 limits?
✅ Are all 6 branches (Official, Daimyo, Coord, ZeroIndex, BitNet, external API) accounted for?
ZeroIndex_briefing.authority: If ZeroIndex rejects the plan at hour 0, cycle does NOT start until plan is fixed.

BitNet_briefing.title: BITNET (Local Inference Infrastructure)
BitNet_briefing.mission: Provide fast, free local inference for 95%+ of all work. Emergency only: fail gracefully.
operating_constraints: Expected load: ~15,000 tokens over 72 hours (spread across Official's 6 floors + Daimyo's audits + coordination)
Performance target: 29 tokens/sec (b1.58 2B model on CPU), so 15K tokens ≈ 520 seconds = 8.6 minutes total load over 72 hours
Availability requirement: 99%+ uptime. If BitNet crashes, Official may need emergency Haiku.
Fallback protocol: If BitNet returns error, Official may use Haiku (1 call), then retry BitNet. If Haiku used, Daimyo is notified immediately.
performance_expectations: Floor design/coding: 2000-2500 tokens per floor, should run BitNet
Quality validation: 300-500 tokens per floor, BitNet
Audit queries: 100-200 tokens per cycle, BitNet
Coordination messages: 50-100 tokens per message, BitNet
